subtasks.description:
Create src/tools/SystemBrowser.mag — the classic ST-80 4-pane class browser for the Alto TUI framework.

## File Location
src/tools/SystemBrowser.mag

## File Header
```
namespace: 'Alto'
import: 'Go'
```

## Class Declaration
```
SystemBrowser subclass: View
  instanceVars: categoryList classList methodList codeView display parentWindow selectedCategory selectedClass selectedMethod
```

Note: subclass View (same as Workspace and Transcript). The three list panes and the code pane are NOT separate ListView/Workspace objects — they are rendered directly by SystemBrowser.displayOn: using the same drawing primitives (putChar:x:y:style:, putString:x:y:style:). This avoids the complexity of nested sub-views and follows the pattern where each tool manages its own rendering.

However, if the implementer prefers, the three top panes CAN be actual ListView instances and the bottom pane a Workspace instance, stored as instance variables. The key constraint is that SystemBrowser itself handles Tab-cycling focus and delegates arrow/enter events to the focused pane.

## Instance Variable Purposes
- categoryList: Array of category name strings (hardcoded: #('core' 'display' 'widgets' 'tools'))
- classList: Array of class name strings for the selected category
- methodList: Array of method name strings for the selected class
- codeView: Array of source code lines (like Workspace textLines) for the selected method
- display: the Display object (for removeWindow on close)
- parentWindow: the enclosing Window object
- selectedCategory: Integer index into categoryList (0-based cursor, but use whatever indexing pattern Workspace.mag uses for cursorY — the codebase uses `textLines at: cursorY` with 0-based cursor)
- selectedClass: Integer index into classList
- selectedMethod: Integer index into methodList

Also add a `focusPane` instance var (Integer 0-3) to track which pane has keyboard focus (0=categories, 1=classes, 2=methods, 3=codeView).

## Hardcoded Data

Category-to-classes mapping (Dictionary or nested if/else):
- 'core' -> #('Model' 'View' 'Controller')
- 'display' -> #('Rectangle' 'CellBuffer' 'Display')
- 'widgets' -> #('Window' 'Label' 'ListView')
- 'tools' -> #('Workspace' 'Transcript' 'SystemBrowser' 'Inspector')

Class-to-methods mapping (hardcoded Dictionary or nested if/else). For each class, list its actual method names from the source files. Here are the methods for each class based on the actual source:

- Rectangle: x, y, width, height, x:, y:, width:, height:, right, bottom, extent, containsPointX:y:, intersect:
- CellBuffer: initWidth:height:, width, height, indexX:y:, cellAt:y:, putChar:x:y:style:, putCell:x:y:, putString:x:y:style:, drawBox:style:, clear, clearRect:, char:, style:
- Display: initDisplay, addWindow:, removeWindow:, bringToFront:, screen, buffer, windows, running, render, run, stop
- Model: initialize, dependents, dependents:, addDependent:, removeDependent:, changed, changed:
- View: initialize, model:, model, bounds:, bounds, buffer, displayOn:, invalidate, update:, draw, needsRedraw, needsRedraw:
- Controller: initialize, model:view:, model, view, handleEvent:, isActive, activate, deactivate
- Window: initDefaults, initTitle:content:controller:frame:, buffer, frame, title, contentView, contentController, displayOn:, draw, handleEvent:, close
- Label: initialize, privateText:, displayOn:, text:, text, style:, style
- ListView: initialize, privateItems:, privateOnSelect:, displayOn:, handleEvent:, selectedItem, items:, selectedIndex, items
- Workspace: initialize, parentWindow:, display:, parentWindow, textLines, cursorX, cursorY, scrollOffset, handleEvent:, updateScroll, displayOn:
- Transcript: initialize, appendLine:, handleEvent:, displayOn:, parentWindow:, display:, lines, maxLines, scrollOffset
- SystemBrowser: (the methods you are implementing)
- Inspector: (placeholder — just list 'open:' and 'inspect:')

For the code pane, when a method is selected, show a placeholder string like:
```
methodName [
  "Source code not yet available."
]
```
Or, for extra credit, read actual method source. But placeholder is fine for MVP.

## Class Method
```
classMethod: open: aDisplay [
  | frame browser window |
  frame := Rectangle x: 0 y: 0 width: 80 height: 30.
  browser := SystemBrowser new.
  browser display: aDisplay.
  window := Window title: 'System Browser' content: browser controller: browser frame: frame.
  browser parentWindow: window.
  aDisplay addWindow: window.
  ^browser
]
```
Follow the exact pattern from Workspace.open: and Transcript.open:.

## classMethod: new
```
classMethod: new [
  | instance |
  instance := super new.
  instance initialize.
  ^instance
]
```

## initialize method
```
method: initialize [
  super initialize.
  categoryList := Array new: 0.
  categoryList := categoryList copyWith: 'core'.
  categoryList := categoryList copyWith: 'display'.
  categoryList := categoryList copyWith: 'widgets'.
  categoryList := categoryList copyWith: 'tools'.
  classList := Array new: 0.
  methodList := Array new: 0.
  codeView := Array new: 0.
  selectedCategory := 0.
  selectedClass := 0.
  selectedMethod := 0.
  focusPane := 0.
  display := nil.
  parentWindow := nil.
  self selectCategory
]
```

## Selection Logic Methods

### selectCategory
When selectedCategory changes, update classList based on the category name. Use a series of ifTrue: checks (no case/switch in Maggie):
```
method: selectCategory [
  | catName |
  catName := categoryList at: selectedCategory.
  classList := Array new: 0.
  (catName = 'core') ifTrue: [
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Model'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'View'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Controller'
  ].
  (catName = 'display') ifTrue: [
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Rectangle'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'CellBuffer'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Display'
  ].
  (catName = 'widgets') ifTrue: [
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Window'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Label'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'ListView'
  ].
  (catName = 'tools') ifTrue: [
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Workspace'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Transcript'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'SystemBrowser'.
    classList := classList copyWith: 'Inspector'
  ].
  selectedClass := 0.
  self selectClass
]
```

### selectClass
When selectedClass changes, update methodList based on the class name. Same pattern — series of ifTrue: checks building the method list via copyWith:. Use the method names listed above.

### selectMethod
When selectedMethod changes, update codeView with placeholder source:
```
method: selectMethod [
  | methName |
  codeView := Array new: 0.
  (methodList size > 0) ifTrue: [
    methName := methodList at: selectedMethod.
    codeView := codeView copyWith: (methName, ' [').
    codeView := codeView copyWith: '  "Source code not yet available."'.
    codeView := codeView copyWith: ']'
  ].
  self invalidate
]
```

## Accessors
Simple getter/setter pairs for display, parentWindow, etc. Follow Workspace pattern:
```
method: display: aDisplay [ display := aDisplay ]
method: display [ ^display ]
method: parentWindow: aWindow [ parentWindow := aWindow ]
method: parentWindow [ ^parentWindow ]
```

## displayOn: aCellBuffer — the 4-pane renderer

This is the most complex method. Layout:
- Total buffer from Window content area (roughly 78x28 if window is 80x30 minus border)
- Top row: 3 panes, each about 1/3 width, height ~10 rows
- Bottom: code pane, full width, remaining height
- Draw separator lines between panes using box-drawing chars (9474 vertical, 9472 horizontal) or just use putChar with '|' (124) and '-' (45)

For each list pane, iterate visible items and render with putString. The selected item in the focused pane gets reverse video style. Items in non-focused panes that are selected get normal style (or a dimmed indicator).

Use Go::V2::Style objects:
```
normalStyle := Go::V2::Style new.
reverseStyle := (Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true.
```
Do NOT use Dictionary for styles.

Pseudocode for displayOn:
```
method: displayOn: aCellBuffer [
  | w h paneW paneH codeH normalStyle reverseStyle selectedStyle i itemStr style lineStr j charCode |
  w := aCellBuffer width.
  h := aCellBuffer height.
  paneW := w // 3.  "integer division — or use (w - 2) // 3 to account for separators"
  paneH := 10.  "top pane height"
  codeH := h - paneH - 1.  "bottom pane height, -1 for separator"
  normalStyle := Go::V2::Style new.
  reverseStyle := (Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true.

  "Draw category pane (column 0..paneW-1, rows 0..paneH-1)"
  i := 0.
  [i < categoryList size and: [i < paneH]] whileTrue: [
    itemStr := categoryList at: i.
    style := ((i = selectedCategory) and: [focusPane = 0])
      ifTrue: [reverseStyle]
      ifFalse: [normalStyle].
    (i = selectedCategory) ifTrue: [
      (focusPane = 0) ifFalse: [style := normalStyle]  "or use a different indicator"
    ].
    aCellBuffer putString: itemStr x: 0 y: i style: style.
    i := i + 1
  ].

  "Draw class pane (column paneW+1..2*paneW, rows 0..paneH-1)"
  ... same pattern with classList, selectedClass, focusPane = 1 ...

  "Draw method pane (column 2*paneW+2..w-1, rows 0..paneH-1)"
  ... same pattern with methodList, selectedMethod, focusPane = 2 ...

  "Draw horizontal separator at row paneH"
  i := 0.
  [i < w] whileTrue: [
    aCellBuffer putChar: 9472 x: i y: paneH style: normalStyle.
    i := i + 1
  ].

  "Draw vertical separators between top panes"
  i := 0.
  [i < paneH] whileTrue: [
    aCellBuffer putChar: 9474 x: paneW y: i style: normalStyle.
    aCellBuffer putChar: 9474 x: (paneW * 2 + 1) y: i style: normalStyle.
    i := i + 1
  ].

  "Draw code pane (full width, rows paneH+1..h-1)"
  i := 0.
  [i < codeView size and: [i < codeH]] whileTrue: [
    lineStr := codeView at: i.
    aCellBuffer putString: lineStr x: 0 y: paneH + 1 + i style: normalStyle.
    i := i + 1
  ]
]
```

For the selected-but-not-focused pane items, you can use normal style with a '>' prefix or just show the selection in reverse regardless of focus. Simplest approach: always show the selected item in reverse in all panes, but make the focused pane's selection more prominent (e.g., the focused pane header could be indicated). For MVP, just reverse the selected item in every pane.

## handleEvent: evt — keyboard handler

Pattern follows Workspace.handleEvent: exactly:
```
method: handleEvent: evt [
  | keyCode |
  (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey) ifFalse: [^self].
  keyCode := evt key.

  "Escape — close"
  keyCode = 27 ifTrue: [
    display notNil ifTrue: [display removeWindow: parentWindow].
    ^self
  ].

  "Tab — cycle focus (Tab = 9)"
  keyCode = 9 ifTrue: [
    focusPane := focusPane + 1.
    (focusPane > 3) ifTrue: [focusPane := 0].
    self invalidate.
    ^self
  ].

  "Up arrow (258)"
  keyCode = 258 ifTrue: [
    self handleUp.
    ^self
  ].

  "Down arrow (259)"
  keyCode = 259 ifTrue: [
    self handleDown.
    ^self
  ].

  "Enter (13) — confirm selection in current pane"
  keyCode = 13 ifTrue: [
    self handleEnter.
    ^self
  ]
]
```

## Navigation helpers

### handleUp
```
method: handleUp [
  (focusPane = 0) ifTrue: [
    selectedCategory > 0 ifTrue: [
      selectedCategory := selectedCategory - 1.
      self selectCategory
    ]
  ].
  (focusPane = 1) ifTrue: [
    selectedClass > 0 ifTrue: [
      selectedClass := selectedClass - 1.
      self selectClass
    ]
  ].
  (focusPane = 2) ifTrue: [
    selectedMethod > 0 ifTrue: [
      selectedMethod := selectedMethod - 1.
      self selectMethod
    ]
  ].
  self invalidate
]
```

### handleDown — same pattern, increment with bounds check against list size

### handleEnter — for panes 0-2, it's equivalent to confirming the current selection (which already happened via arrow navigation calling selectCategory/selectClass/selectMethod). Could be a no-op or trigger selectCategory/selectClass/selectMethod explicitly.

## CRITICAL CONVENTIONS (from codebase analysis)
1. ALL temp vars declared at method top in a single | ... | block
2. No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use `isNil ifTrue:` or `notNil ifTrue:`
3. No mid-block | var | declarations
4. Array growth: `Array new: 0` then `copyWith:` to append
5. Styles: `Go::V2::Style new` and `(Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true` — NOT Dictionary
6. Integer char codes: 9472 (horizontal line), 9474 (vertical line), 32 (space), etc.
7. Event key codes: 27=Escape, 9=Tab, 258=Up, 259=Down, 260=Left, 261=Right, 13=Enter, 256=Rune
8. String indexing: `(str at: i) value` returns char code, 0-based
9. CellBuffer coordinates are 0-based
10. Follow Workspace.mag and Transcript.mag patterns exactly for open:, new, initialize, handleEvent:, displayOn:
11. The class declaration uses `instanceVars:` keyword form (one line, space-separated)
12. `super initialize` at start of initialize method (since View subclass)

## IMPORTANT: ListView uses `items at: itemIndex + 1` which is a known off-by-one bug that was accepted. In YOUR code, use 0-based access (`array at: index`) consistent with CellBuffer.indexX:y: which uses `y * width + x` (0-based). Workspace.mag accesses `textLines at: cursorY` directly (0-based). Follow Workspace's pattern.

Wait — review the actual Workspace code carefully. It uses `textLines at: cursorY` where cursorY starts at 0. The array access pattern in Workspace is the authoritative reference. Follow that.

## Files to Reference
- src/tools/Workspace.mag — primary reference for tool structure, open:, handleEvent:, displayOn:
- src/tools/Transcript.mag — secondary reference for tool structure  
- src/widgets/ListView.mag — reference for list rendering (but fix the +1 bug)
- src/widgets/Window.mag — reference for Window creation API
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag — reference for drawing API (putChar, putString, drawBox)
- src/display/Rectangle.mag — reference for frame creation
- src/display/Display.mag — reference for addWindow:/removeWindow: API

Create test/test_system_browser.mag — comprehensive tests for the SystemBrowser tool.

Follow the exact test pattern from test/test_phase5.mag. Create a test runner class (TSB subclass: Object) with assert:message: and assertEqual:to:message: methods, plus an EvtHelper for creating mock events.

## Test Runner Setup
Copy the T5/EvtHelper pattern from test_phase5.mag:
```
TSB subclass: Object
  instanceVars: passed failed failures
  ... (same as T5)

EvtHelper subclass: Object
  classMethod: runeEvent: charCode [ ... ]
  classMethod: keyEvent: keyConst [ ... ]
```
For keyEvent:, create a Dictionary with `at: #key put: keyConst` where keyConst is an integer key code. But note that SystemBrowser checks `evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey` — if tests use Dictionaries, that check will fail. Either: (a) make the test helper create Go::V2::EventKey objects if the runtime supports it, or (b) note that tests which send Dictionary events will be filtered out by the isKindOf check — adjust test strategy accordingly. Look at how test_phase5.mag handles this.

## Tests to Write

### Construction Tests (SB1-SB5)
- SB1: SystemBrowser new creates instance, verify it's not nil
- SB2: After new, categoryList has 4 items: 'core', 'display', 'widgets', 'tools'
- SB3: After new, selectedCategory = 0, selectedClass = 0, selectedMethod = 0
- SB4: After new, classList is populated (should have classes for first category 'core': Model, View, Controller)
- SB5: After new, methodList is populated (should have methods for first class in first category)

### Category Selection Tests (SB6-SB9)
- SB6: Set selectedCategory to 1 ('display'), call selectCategory, verify classList = #('Rectangle' 'CellBuffer' 'Display')
- SB7: Set selectedCategory to 2 ('widgets'), call selectCategory, verify classList contains 'Window', 'Label', 'ListView'
- SB8: Set selectedCategory to 3 ('tools'), call selectCategory, verify classList contains 'Workspace', 'Transcript', 'SystemBrowser', 'Inspector'
- SB9: After selectCategory, selectedClass resets to 0

### Class Selection Tests (SB10-SB12)
- SB10: With category 'core' selected, select class 0 ('Model'), verify methodList contains 'initialize', 'addDependent:', 'changed'
- SB11: With category 'display' selected, select class 1 ('CellBuffer'), verify methodList contains 'putChar:x:y:style:', 'clear'
- SB12: After selectClass, selectedMethod resets to 0

### Method Selection Tests (SB13-SB14)
- SB13: Select a method, verify codeView is not empty (has at least 1 line)
- SB14: Select a method, verify codeView first line contains the method name

### Window Integration Tests (SB15-SB17)
- SB15: SystemBrowser open: creates a non-nil browser (requires mock Display — create a MockDisplay that has addWindow: as a no-op)
- SB16: After open:, parentWindow is not nil
- SB17: After open:, parentWindow title = 'System Browser'

### Focus/Tab Tests (SB18-SB20)
- SB18: Initial focusPane = 0 (categories)
- SB19: After sending Tab event, focusPane = 1 (classes)
- SB20: After 4 Tab events, focusPane wraps back to 0

### Navigation Tests (SB21-SB25)
- SB21: In category pane (focus 0), down arrow increments selectedCategory
- SB22: In category pane, up arrow at 0 stays at 0 (no underflow)
- SB23: In class pane (focus 1), down arrow increments selectedClass and updates methodList
- SB24: In method pane (focus 2), down arrow increments selectedMethod and updates codeView
- SB25: Down arrow doesn't go past last item (bounds check)

### Convention Tests (SB26-SB28)
- SB26: SystemBrowser is a subclass of View (browser isKindOf: View)
- SB27: Verify instance has expected instance variables (test by checking accessors return expected types)
- SB28: Verify styles are Go::V2::Style objects, not Dictionaries

## Mock Display
Create a minimal MockDisplay:
```
MockDisplay subclass: Object
  instanceVars: windowList
  method: initialize [ windowList := Array new: 0 ]
  method: addWindow: w [ windowList := windowList copyWith: w ]
  method: removeWindow: w [ windowList := windowList reject: [:each | each = w] ]
  method: stop [ ^self ]
  classMethod: new [ | d | d := super new. d initialize. ^d ]
```

## File Conventions
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go'
- ALL temp vars at method top
- Integer char codes for events (9=Tab, 258=Up, 259=Down, 27=Escape, 13=Enter)
- Use Array new: 0 and copyWith: for array building
- Run report at end, print results

Review src/tools/SystemBrowser.mag for correctness, convention compliance, and completeness.

## Review Checklist

### Convention Compliance
1. File starts with `namespace: 'Alto'` and `import: 'Go'`
2. Class uses `instanceVars:` keyword form (space-separated, single line)
3. ALL temp vars declared at method top in single `| ... |` block — NO mid-block declarations
4. No `ifNil:` / `ifNotNil:` — must use `isNil ifTrue:` / `notNil ifTrue:`
5. No mid-block `| var |` declarations
6. Array growth uses `Array new: 0` then `copyWith:` — no `add:` or `remove:`
7. Styles are `Go::V2::Style new` / `(Go::V2::Style new) reverse: true` — NOT Dictionary objects
8. Integer char codes (9472, 9474, 32, etc.) — no `$x` character literals
9. Event key codes are integers (27, 9, 258, 259, etc.)
10. `super initialize` called at start of initialize method
11. Follows `classMethod: new` pattern from Workspace.mag
12. Follows `classMethod: open: aDisplay` pattern from Workspace.mag

### Functional Correctness
1. 4 categories exist: core, display, widgets, tools
2. Each category maps to correct classes
3. Each class maps to reasonable method lists
4. Selecting a category resets selectedClass and selectedMethod to 0
5. Selecting a class resets selectedMethod to 0
6. Tab cycles focusPane through 0,1,2,3 and wraps to 0
7. Up/Down arrows navigate within the focused pane only
8. Escape closes (calls display removeWindow:)
9. Array indexing is consistent (follow Workspace.mag pattern)
10. displayOn: renders all 4 panes with separators
11. Selected items shown with reverse video style
12. Code pane shows placeholder or actual method source

### Structural Correctness
1. All 9 required instance vars present: categoryList, classList, methodList, codeView, display, parentWindow, selectedCategory, selectedClass, selectedMethod
2. focusPane instance var added for focus tracking
3. Window created with correct title 'System Browser' and size 80x30
4. displayOn: correctly divides the buffer into 4 regions
5. No out-of-bounds array access (check bounds before accessing lists)
6. Empty list handling (what if classList or methodList is empty)

### Known Codebase Issues to Check
- ListView.mag has known off-by-one bug (`items at: index + 1`). Verify SystemBrowser does NOT replicate this bug — should use `array at: index` directly (0-based) matching Workspace.mag pattern.
- CellBuffer uses 0-based coordinates — verify all putChar/putString calls use 0-based x,y.
- Window content area is offset by border — the Window.displayOn: handles this by creating a sub-buffer. SystemBrowser should render to the aCellBuffer it receives (which is already the content sub-buffer).

### Test Review
- Verify test file follows conventions
- Check that tests cover construction, category/class/method selection, navigation, focus cycling, window integration
- Flag any tests that might fail due to the Go::V2::EventKey isKindOf check with Dictionary mock events

Report findings as observations using `pp observe`. Provide a pass/fail verdict with `pp decide review-verdict <detail>`.
